model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: UMBC gets the nod because its predictive profile is modestly better in both KenPom and Torvik, with a stronger offense and slightly better overall efficiency than Howard. Howard has a real path if it controls tempo and leans on its better defensive profile, but UMBC’s stronger full-season record and slightly cleaner overall metrics make the Retrievers the safer pick in a close play-in game.

reasoningStep.title: UMBC over Howard in a close First Four matchup
reasoningStep.summary: UMBC holds a small but consistent edge in the major predictive ratings, driven mostly by the better offense, while Howard’s defense keeps the upset risk real. With no major availability issue found for either side, the overall profile points narrowly to UMBC.
evidence: KenPom rates UMBC 185th and Howard 207th, with UMBC holding the better overall adjusted efficiency margin.
Torvik also favors UMBC, 185th to 206th, and shows a notably stronger offensive ranking for the Retrievers.
Howard’s defense grades better than UMBC’s, which gives the Bison a credible upset path if the game slows down.
UMBC finished 24-8 compared with Howard at 23-10, reinforcing the slight edge in full-season performance.
No notable injury or availability news surfaced in the searches for either team.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
